title: Oplismenus najadus
taxonomy: Oplismenus najadus is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Oplismenus and is further classified within the tribe Paniceae. The classification of Oplismenus najadus is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Monocots, Clade: Commelinids, Order: Poales, Family: Poaceae, Tribe: Paniceae, Genus: Oplismenus, Species: O. najadus. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other grasses and its position within the plant kingdom. Understanding Oplismenus najadus' classification is essential for botanists and researchers studying plant diversity and evolution.

intro: Oplismenus najadus, also known as Nettle-leaved Flaxwort, is a perennial plant species in the family Poaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Oplismenus najadus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Oplismenus najadus is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin care.
